title: Pergola Outdoor Room
summary: A large walk-in pergola on four 6x6 posts with built-in bench seating and a decorative lattice back wall — a major permanent structure, distinct from this catalog's smaller arbor bench.
description:
This is a genuinely large-scale structure — a full outdoor room built on four 6x6 posts spaced 10 feet apart, with built-in bench seating along the sides and a decorative lattice back wall, rather than the smaller garden-bench-and-trellis combination this catalog's arbor bench offers. **This project requires the posts to be set in concrete footings below your local frost line** before any of the above-ground framing begins; that groundwork isn't detailed in this plan and needs to be sized to your soil and climate.

Space the posts carefully on a level surface and check that they're parallel at both top and bottom before attaching the long rafters — a structure this size amplifies any out-of-parallel error across its full 10-foot span. Cross supports tie the rafters and posts together next, and the built-in bench shelves attach directly to both the posts and the framing above the seat, so the seating is structural to the pergola rather than a separate add-on.

The decorative rafter tails are optional and shaped with a jigsaw, then notched with repeated circular-saw passes and cleaned up with a chisel — a genuinely more advanced technique than most of this catalog's projects. The lattice back wall builds last: vertical strips stapled to a 2x6 frame, then horizontal strips woven across them at a consistent 3" spacing, trimmed out with more 2x6 at the inside edges. **Given the size and permanence of this structure, check local building codes and setback requirements before starting** — a structure this large may require a permit in many jurisdictions.
timeLabel: 15–24 hrs across multiple days, plus footing cure time

cutList.note: Set into concrete footings below your local frost line.
Optional decorative tails, jigsawed and notched.

steps.title: Set the posts
steps.body: Set the four 6x6 posts in concrete footings below your local frost line, spaced 120" apart outside to outside. Check plumb and let the concrete cure fully before continuing.

steps.title: Attach the rafters
steps.body: Mark the rafters 10-1/2" in from each end. Predrill and attach two posts per rafter with 3-1/2" exterior decking screws, checking the posts stay parallel at top and bottom.

steps.title: Add the cross supports
steps.body: Check the posts are parallel, then attach the cross supports to the posts and rafters with exterior deck screws, pocket holes, or metal brackets.

steps.title: Build the bench shelves
steps.body: Attach the shelf supports to the bench shelf boards, then attach the assembled shelves to the posts through both the supports and the boards.

steps.title: Add the bench end pieces and seat boards
steps.body: Attach the bench end pieces to the ends of the shelf boards. Lay the seat boards in place and attach to the framing below.

steps.title: Add the decorative upper rafters
steps.body: Optionally shape the rafter tails with a jigsaw and notch with repeated circular saw passes, cleaning up with a chisel. Attach the upper rafters to the posts with pocket holes.

steps.title: Build the lattice back wall
steps.body: Attach the lattice frame to the posts with pocket holes. Staple the vertical lattice strips to the frame, starting 1-1/2" offset from center and working outward. Weave the horizontal strips across the vertical ones with 3" spacing. Trim the inside edges with the lattice trim boards.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand and apply an exterior sealer or stain to the entire structure.

unresolvedImages.alt: Large pergola with built-in bench seating and a lattice back wall
